description: All sorts of organic substances rendered into a nutritious goop that can be used as a growth medium in grub aquaculture.  Public material presents it as a closed-loop food recycling product; less flattering rumors say the formula began as industrial waste-stream bioremediation research at Helix Harvest Biotechnologies, an insect-protein startup funded by Trans-Coast Logistics.  Whatever its origin, mealgrubs thrive within it.

A bottle of proprietary mealgrub seed culture sold under the cleaner-sounding trade name "spores".  Mealgrubs are engineered to turn nearly any organic matter into safe edible animal protein, a selling point that made them attractive to disaster logistics planners and food-tech investors.  The company line calls them modified mealworms; rumor says the stock also drew on fly larvae, nematophagous fungi, and other biological samples better left off the label.

Growth medium seeded with mealgrub eggs.  Once placed in a growing vat, they should be ready for harvesting in around 76 hours.  The immature grubs' metabolism can produce toxic by-products, so caution is advisable.

Thousands of mature mealgrubs squirm in the viscous remains of their growth medium.  They are ready for harvest, and require speedy cleaning and processing, as they can quickly rot in this stage.  They look somewhat disgusting: pale, soft, and uncomfortably maggot-like.  According to product literature this is normal: mealgrubs remain in a sterile juvenile form for their entire useful lifespan and die quickly once removed from the vat.

A handful of edible, heavily genemodded grubs, each only slightly thinner than your thumb.  Advertisements call them sustainable mealworm protein, but they look uncomfortably like pale maggots, with soft bodies and no sign they were ever meant to become beetles.

Removed from their growth solution they quickly die and desiccate, as these already have.  They could be safely eaten with no further processing; if you can get past the sight of them, they make a nutritious meal with little odor and a bland.
